title: NOC 12201 – Insurance Adjusters and Claims Examiners | GTR Immigration
description: Insurance Adjusters and Claims Examiners play a crucial role in Canada’s insurance industry by assessing losses, validating claims, and ensuring fair settlements. Classified under NOC 12201, this occupation continues to experience strong demand nationwide and offers clear immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.major_group: 122 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations
classification.minor_group: 1220 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations

common_job_titles: Adjuster Claims examiner Claims representative Insurance adjuster Production examiner
duties: Inspect automobile, residential, and commercial property damage
Take statements from claimants, witnesses, and medical professionals
Assess policy coverage and calculate losses
Review and authorize claims investigated by adjusters
Analyze reports and precedents to determine coverage
Approve automobile, fire, disability, dental, and life insurance claims
employment_requirements: A bachelor’s degree, college diploma, or significant insurance-related experience
Industry courses and several years of on-the-job training
Provincial licensing for independent adjusters through the Superintendent of Insurance
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 29,900 new positions for Insurance Adjusters and Claims Examiners, compared with 24,900 job seekers—making this occupation particularly attractive for skilled immigrants with insurance-sector experience.
additional_information: Continued hiring across property, casualty, and life insurance sectorsStable demand following increased claims activity and regulatory oversightEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $39,500 to $96,300 CAD
